body_txt: Dancing has been a fundamental part of human expression for what's likely a very long time — possibly even longer. For most people, dancing is simply a form of entertainment or physical exercise. But for some, dancing is more than just that: it's a way of life. These characters don't just dance as a job or obligation, they love to do so, regardless if there's any music playing in the background — or if there's anyone else around, for that matter. If dancing signifies joy and celebration, then these characters naturally embody those traits wholeheartedly, being generally optimistic and enthusiastic in their demeanor, sometimes to the extent of being Fun Personified , along with possibly encouraging others to dance along with them. While a character who loves to dance can naturally utilize said talent for some purpose, merely having that ability and using it for whatever reason doesn't count for the trope unless they explicitly have a liking for the act itself. Such characters are sometimes Dancing Royalty , though these characters don't have the requirement of being exceptionally good at dancing and simply enjoy the act nonetheless. Can also be a Dance Battler if their passion for dancing overlaps with their capabilities as a fighter. Compare Happy Dance , which is when somebody expresses their happiness/enthusiasm by dancing. Contrast I Can't Dance . If they like to dance but are bad at it, it might overlap with Sucks at Dancing . Truth in Television for some people, but to avoid bloating the page with every person who's ever shown a prominent interest in dancing, Real Life examples are excluded.
